pages.title: Finding Traders
pages.text: Traders are hand-placed by an admin. They don't generate naturally — on a fresh server you may need to ask where they live, or wait until one is set up.$(br2)They are invulnerable, anchored (no walking, no knockback), and never take damage.

pages.title: Combat gear traders
pages.text: $(bold)Wizard$(/) — wands, staves, robes, books.$(br2)$(bold)Bishop, Priest$(/) — holy plate, holy wands.$(br2)$(bold)Archer$(/) — bows, crossbows, spears, quiver.$(br2)$(bold)Rogue$(/) — rogue and assassin armor, grave-sentinel set.$(br2)$(bold)Blacksmith$(/) — generic iron, gold, diamond weapons.$(br2)$(bold)Armorer, Frost Knight$(/) — generic plate, death-knight gear.

pages.title: Themed armor and engineers
pages.text: $(bold)Nomad, Ronin, Centurion, Wanderer, Pirate$(/) — themed plate.$(br2)$(bold)Engineer$(/) — Immersive Aircraft parts.$(br2)$(bold)Mechanist$(/) — Create machines, Create Additions electrics.$(br2)$(bold)Tinkerer$(/) — tech gadgets.$(br2)$(bold)Fisherman$(/) — fishing tackle.$(br2)$(bold)Arms Merchant$(/) — firearms. The Pistol is cheaper at the Pirate trader.

pages.title: Cosmetic and infrastructure
pages.text: $(bold)Musician$(/) — instruments.$(br2)$(bold)Banker$(/) — Numismatics infrastructure (cards, terminals, vendor, depositors). Raw cogs come from $(l:factions/treasurer)Treasurer$(/l) only.$(br2)$(bold)Flag Maker$(/) — the six themed Mine Cells flags.$(br2)$(bold)Decorator, Tailor, Curiosities$(/) — cosmetics.$(br2)$(bold)Librarian$(/) — spell books, journals, canvases.$(br2)$(bold)Grandmaster$(/) — chess set.$(br2)$(bold)Cultist, Scientist, Blight$(/) — black-bridge-priced death-knight gear.$(br2)$(bold)Dealer$(/) — Nirvana fun trader, Prison-only, items also normally craftable.
